response.justification: Szabo is a talented, versatile engineer with strong fundamentals in distributed systems infrastructure and technical mentoring. However, he critically falls short on the minimum 8-year C++ requirement, appearing to have only ~1-2 years of C++ experience. His career has been generalist across multiple languages (JavaScript, TypeScript, DLang, Java) rather than deep specialist in C++-based systems. While his infrastructure experience with high-scale microservices and Kubernetes is relevant, his recent focus on AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling suggests different technical interests than a C++-heavy cloud compute platform leadership role demands.
strengths: Demonstrated experience leading high-scale distributed systems handling millions of requests per second with microservices architecture
Proven technical mentoring capability through organizing workshops, code katas, and teaching programs at multiple companies
Strong software craftsmanship foundation emphasizing testing, TDD, CI/CD, and architectural design patterns
concerns: Only ~1-2 years of C++ experience versus the non-negotiable 8-year minimum requirement
Career spans diverse tech stacks with no deep specialization in C++-based systems or cloud compute platforms
Technical leadership roles lack demonstrated sustained responsibility for cloud/DevOps team strategy or architecture decisions
